name: Recursive Directory Enumeration via cmd.exe (Pre-Ransomware Recon)
description: Detects cmd.exe invoked with /s (recurse) and /b (bare format) flags — the canonical pre-encryption file system enumeration pattern used by ransomware operators to identify valuable files prior to encryption. The bare format flag suppresses all output except raw paths, indicating automated non-interactive use.
query: process where process.name == "cmd.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*dir*" and process.command_line like~ "*/s*" and process.command_line like~ "*/b*"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Multi-Extension File Search Burst (Ransomware Target Profiling)
description: Detects a burst of cmd.exe processes issuing dir commands targeting ransomware-relevant file extensions (.docx, .xlsx, .pdf, .db, .sql, .bak) within a 2-minute window. Ransomware operators and pre-encryption automation issue one extension query per file type to build a comprehensive target inventory. Five or more such queries in rapid succession is a high-confidence ransomware pre-encryption indicator.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=2m
  [process where process.name == "cmd.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*dir*" and process.command_line like~ "*.docx*"]
  [process where process.name == "cmd.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*dir*" and process.command_line like~ "*.xlsx*"]
  [process where process.name == "cmd.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*dir*" and process.command_line like~ "*.pdf*"]
  [process where process.name == "cmd.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*dir*" and (process.command_line like~ "*.db*" or process.command_line like~ "*.sql*" or process.command_line like~ "*.bak*")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Seatbelt GhostPack Comprehensive Host Enumeration
description: Detects execution of Seatbelt (GhostPack offensive toolkit) with any command-line arguments. Seatbelt performs 60+ host security checks including credential enumeration (WindowsCredentialFiles, WindowsVault, DPAPI), system configuration, and network state. The -group=all argument triggers all checks simultaneously. Used by APT groups, ransomware operators, and red teams for pre-attack host profiling. Zero tolerance — any instance should be investigated.
query: process where process.name == "Seatbelt.exe" or (process.command_line like~ "*-group=all*" and process.command_line like~ "*Seatbelt*") or process.command_line like~ "*WindowsCredentialFiles*WindowsVault*" or process.command_line like~ "*WindowsVault*InterestingFiles*"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Credential Store File Access by Non-System Process
description: Detects non-system processes accessing Windows Credential Manager storage locations (AppData\Microsoft\Credentials, Vault, Protect directories). These paths contain DPAPI-protected credential blobs, Windows Vault entries, and master key files. Legitimate access is restricted to lsass.exe, svchost.exe, and the credential UI subsystem. Access from other processes indicates credential harvesting reconnaissance as performed by Seatbelt's WindowsCredentialFiles and WindowsVault checks, or any equivalent tool.
query: file where file.path like~ "*\\AppData\\*\\Microsoft\\Credentials\\*" or file.path like~ "*\\AppData\\*\\Microsoft\\Protect\\*" or file.path like~ "*\\AppData\\*\\Microsoft\\Vault\\*" and not process.name in ("lsass.exe", "svchost.exe", "taskhostw.exe", "RuntimeBroker.exe", "CredentialUIBroker.exe", "consent.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Pre-Encryption Recon Kill Chain — File Enumeration then Target List Creation
description: High-confidence composite detection for pre-encryption ransomware reconnaissance kill chain. Correlates: (1) recursive dir enumeration with /s /b flags, followed by (2) creation of a text file by a cmd.exe or PowerShell child process within 5 minutes on the same host. This two-step sequence — enumerate then stage — is the defining behavioral pattern of pre-encryption ransomware recon. Very few legitimate workflows exhibit both behaviors in sequence.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m
  [process where process.name == "cmd.exe" and process.command_line like~ "*dir*" and process.command_line like~ "*/s*" and process.command_line like~ "*/b*"]
  [file where file.extension == "txt" and file.name != "" and not process.name in ("notepad.exe", "wordpad.exe", "code.exe")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
